methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $274M - $1.3B.

data.narrative:
Albedo is a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised $130M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 same-stage private peers in the space sector—Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M)—alongside a broader public reference set of 5 aerospace tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median 59.8x price-to-sales.

Two quantitative methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $247M. The peer-median method across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $400M, with a range spanning $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue data, a standard constraint for private company analysis. The two applicable methods triangulate to a range of $210M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $324M.

The analysis supports a valuation centered near $324M, bounded by methodological conservatism at $210M and peer-set dispersion at $460M. Key uncertainties include revenue scale and trajectory (unspecified), customer concentration, and competitive positioning relative to Aalyria, which commands a significant valuation premium within the peer set.
